info.description: Curated subset of the real, published Atlassian spec (source: pagerduty.json), trimmed to the operations Anvil backtests against a mature reference MCP server.
info.title: PagerDuty REST API (curated subset)

get.description:
List all of the existing escalation policies.

Escalation policies define which user should be alerted at which time.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#escalation-policies)

Scoped OAuth requires: `escalation_policies.read`

200.description: A paginated array of escalation policy objects.

get.description:
Get information about an existing escalation policy and its rules.

Escalation policies define which user should be alerted at which time.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#escalation-policies)

Scoped OAuth requires: `escalation_policies.read`

escalation_policy.name: Another Escalation Policy
escalation_policy.description: This is yet another escalation policy

get.description:
List existing incidents.

An incident represents a problem or an issue that needs to be addressed and resolved.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#incidents)

Scoped OAuth requires: `incidents.read`

post.description:
Create an incident synchronously without a corresponding event from a monitoring service.

An incident represents a problem or an issue that needs to be addressed and resolved.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#incidents)

Scoped OAuth requires: `incidents.write`

This API operation has operation specific rate limits. See the [Rate Limits](https://developer.pagerduty.com/docs/72d3b724589e3-rest-api-rate-limits) page for more information.

incident.description: Details of the incident to be created.

title.description: A succinct description of the nature, symptoms, cause, or effect of the incident.

incident_key.description: A string which identifies the incident. Sending subsequent requests referencing the same service and with the same incident_key will result in those requests being rejected if an open incident matches that incident_key.

assignments.description: Assign the incident to these assignees. Cannot be specified if an escalation policy is given.

body.details: A disk is getting full on this machine. You should investigate what is causing the disk to fill, and ensure that there is an automated process in place for ensuring data is rotated (eg. logs should have logrotate around them). If data is expected to stay on this disk forever, you should start planning to scale up to a larger disk.

get.description:
Show detailed information about an incident. Accepts either an incident id, or an incident number.

An incident represents a problem or an issue that needs to be addressed and resolved.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#incidents)

Scoped OAuth requires: `incidents.read`

incidents_responders.message: Please help with issue - join bridge at +1(234)-567-8910

responder_requests.message: Please help with issue - join bridge at +1(234)-567-8910

incidents_responders.message: Please help with issue - join bridge at +1(234)-567-8910

put.description:
Acknowledge, resolve, escalate or reassign an incident.

An incident represents a problem or an issue that needs to be addressed and resolved.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#incidents)

Scoped OAuth requires: `incidents.write`

incident.description: The parameters of the incident to update.

status.description: The new status of the incident. If the incident is currently resolved, setting the status to "triggered" or "acknowledged" will reopen it. When reopening an incident to the "triggered" status, it will be assigned based on the assignees or escalation_policy fields in the request, otherwise it will be assigned to the current Escalation Policy. When reopening an incident to the "acknowledged" status, it will be assigned to the current user.

priority.description: The priority of the incident. Can be provided as a priority object or a string matching a priority name. If a string is provided, the highest priority with a matching name will be used.

name.description: The user-provided short name of the priority.

oneOf.description: A string matching the name of a priority. If provided, the highest priority with a matching name will be used.

resolution.description:
The resolution for this incident. This field is used only when setting the incident status to resolved.
The value provided here is added to the incident’s 'Resolve' log entry as a note and will not be displayed directly in the UI.

title.description: The new title of the incident.

escalation_level.description: Escalate the incident to this level in the escalation policy.

assignments.description: Assign the incident to these assignees.

service.description: Assign the incident to this service.

get.description:
List the on-call entries during a given time range.

An on-call represents a contiguous unit of time for which a User will be on call for a given Escalation Policy and Escalation Rules.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#on-calls)

Scoped OAuth requires: `oncalls.read`

This API operation has operation specific rate limits. See the [Rate Limits](https://developer.pagerduty.com/docs/72d3b724589e3-rest-api-rate-limits) page for more information.

200.description: A paginated array of on-call objects.

get.description:
List the on-call schedules.

A Schedule determines the time periods that users are On-Call.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#schedules)

Scoped OAuth requires: `schedules.read`

200.description: A paginated array of schedule objects.

get.description:
Show detailed information about a schedule, including entries for each layer.
Scoped OAuth requires: `schedules.read`

List existing Services.

A service may represent an application, component, or team you wish to open incidents against.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#services)

Scoped OAuth requires: `services.read`

Get details about an existing service.

A service may represent an application, component, or team you wish to open incidents against.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#services)

Scoped OAuth requires: `services.read`

List users of your PagerDuty account, optionally filtered by a search query.

Users are members of a PagerDuty account that have the ability to interact with Incidents and other data on the account.

For more information see the [API Concepts Document](../../api-reference/a47605517c19a-api-concepts#users)

Scoped OAuth requires: `users.read`

offset_limit.name: limit
offset_limit.description: The number of results per page.

offset_offset.name: offset
offset_offset.description: Offset to start pagination search results.

offset_total.name: total
offset_total.description:
By default the `total` field in pagination responses is set to `null` to provide the fastest possible response times. Set `total` to `true` for this field to be populated.

See our [Pagination Docs](https://developer.pagerduty.com/docs/rest-api-v2/pagination/) for more information.

header_Accept.name: Accept
header_Accept.description: The `Accept` header is used as a versioning header.

query.name: query
query.description: Filters the result, showing only the records whose name matches the query.

user_ids_escalation_policies.name: user_ids[]
user_ids_escalation_policies.description: Filters the results, showing only escalation policies on which any of the users is a target.

team_ids.name: team_ids[]
team_ids.description: An array of team IDs. Only results related to these teams will be returned. Account must have the `teams` ability to use this parameter.

include_escalation_policy.name: include[]
include_escalation_policy.description: Array of additional Models to include in response.

sort_by_escalation_policy.name: sort_by
sort_by_escalation_policy.description: Used to specify the field you wish to sort the results on.

incident_list_limit.name: limit
incident_list_limit.description: The number of results per page. Maximum of 100.

date_range.name: date_range
date_range.description: When set to all, the since and until parameters and defaults are ignored.

incident_key.name: incident_key
incident_key.description: Incident de-duplication key. Incidents with child alerts do not have an incident key; querying by incident key will return incidents whose alerts have alert_key matching the given incident key.

incident_services.name: service_ids[]
incident_services.description: Returns only the incidents associated with the passed service(s). This expects one or more service IDs.

incident_assigned_to_user.name: user_ids[]
incident_assigned_to_user.description: Returns only the incidents currently assigned to the passed user(s). This expects one or more user IDs. Note: When using the assigned_to_user filter, you will only receive incidents with statuses of triggered or acknowledged. This is because resolved incidents are not assigned to any user.

incident_urgencies.name: urgencies[]
incident_urgencies.description: Array of the urgencies of the incidents to be returned. Defaults to all urgencies. Account must have the `urgencies` ability to do this.

time_zone.name: time_zone
time_zone.description: Time zone in which results will be rendered. This will default to the account time zone.

statuses_incidents.name: statuses[]
statuses_incidents.description: Return only incidents with the given statuses. To query multiple statuses, pass `statuses[]` more than once, for example: `https://api.pagerduty.com/incidents?statuses[]=triggered&statuses[]=acknowledged`. (More status codes may be introduced in the future.)

sort_by_incidents.name: sort_by
sort_by_incidents.description: Used to specify both the field you wish to sort the results on (incident_number/created_at/resolved_at/urgency), as well as the direction (asc/desc) of the results. The sort_by field and direction should be separated by a colon. A maximum of two fields can be included, separated by a comma. Sort direction defaults to ascending. The account must have the `urgencies` ability to sort by the urgency.

include_incidents.name: include[]
include_incidents.description: Array of additional details to include.

since_incidents.name: since
since_incidents.description: The start of the date range over which you want to search. Maximum range is 6 months and default is 1 month.

until_incidents.name: until
until_incidents.description: The end of the date range over which you want to search. Maximum range is 6 months and default is 1 month.

from_header.name: From
from_header.description: The email address of a valid user associated with the account making the request.

include_incident.name: include[]
include_incident.description: Array of additional details to include.

include_oncalls.name: include[]
include_oncalls.description: Array of additional details to include.

user_ids_oncalls.name: user_ids[]
user_ids_oncalls.description: Filters the results, showing only on-calls for the specified user IDs.

escalation_policy_ids_oncalls.name: escalation_policy_ids[]
escalation_policy_ids_oncalls.description: Filters the results, showing only on-calls for the specified escalation policy IDs.

schedule_ids_oncalls.name: schedule_ids[]
schedule_ids_oncalls.description: Filters the results, showing only on-calls for the specified schedule IDs. If `null` is provided in the array, it includes permanent on-calls due to direct user escalation targets.

since_oncalls.name: since
since_oncalls.description: The start of the time range over which you want to search. If an on-call period overlaps with the range, it will be included in the result. Defaults to current time. On-call shifts are limited to 90 days in the future.

until_oncalls.name: until
until_oncalls.description: The end of the time range over which you want to search. If an on-call period overlaps with the range, it will be included in the result. Defaults to current time. On-call shifts are limited to 90 days in the future, and the `until` time cannot be before the `since` time.

earliest_oncalls.name: earliest
earliest_oncalls.description: This will filter on-calls such that only the earliest on-call for each combination of escalation policy, escalation level, and user is returned. This is useful for determining when the "next" on-calls are for a given set of filters.

include_schedules.name: include[]
include_schedules.description: Array of additional details to include.

schedule_list_time_zone.name: time_zone
schedule_list_time_zone.description: Time zone in which results will be rendered. This will default to the current user's time zone and then the account's time zone.

include_next_oncall_for_user.name: include_next_oncall_for_user
include_next_oncall_for_user.description: Specify an `user_id`, and the schedule list API will return information about this user's next on-call.

schedule_since.name: since
schedule_since.description: The start of the date range over which you want to show schedule entries. Defaults to 2 weeks before until if an until is given. Optional parameter. When provided with include[] for schedule types, populates the rendered_schedule_entries fields in the response.

schedule_until.name: until
schedule_until.description: The end of the date range over which you want to show schedule entries. Defaults to 2 weeks after since if a since is given. Optional parameter. When provided with include[] for schedule types, populates the rendered_schedule_entries fields in the response.

schedule_time_zone.name: time_zone
schedule_time_zone.description: Time zone in which results will be rendered. This will default to the schedule's time zone.

schedule_overflow.name: overflow
schedule_overflow.description:
Any on-call schedule entries that pass the date range bounds will be truncated at the bounds, unless the parameter `overflow=true` is passed. This parameter defaults to false.
For instance, if your schedule is a rotation that changes daily at midnight UTC, and your date range is from `2011-06-01T10:00:00Z` to `2011-06-01T14:00:00Z`:


- If you don't pass the `overflow=true` parameter, you will get one schedule entry returned with a start of `2011-06-01T10:00:00Z` and end of `2011-06-01T14:00:00Z`.
- If you do pass the `overflow=true` parameter, you will get one schedule entry returned with a start of `2011-06-01T00:00:00Z` and end of `2011-06-02T00:00:00Z`.

sort_by_service.name: sort_by
sort_by_service.description: Used to specify the field you wish to sort the results on.

include_services.name: include[]
include_services.description: Array of additional details to include.

service_name.name: name
service_name.description: Filters the results, showing only services with the specified name.

include_services_id.name: include[]
include_services_id.description: Array of additional details to include.

include_user.name: include[]
include_user.description: Array of additional Models to include in response.

more.description: Indicates if there are additional records to return

total.description: The total number of records matching the given query.

type.description: The type of object being created.

name.description: The name of the escalation policy.

num_loops.description: The number of times the escalation policy will repeat after reaching the end of its escalation.

on_call_handoff_notifications.description: Determines how on call handoff notifications will be sent for users on the escalation policy. Defaults to "if_has_services".

teams.description: Team associated with the policy. Account must have the `teams` ability to use this parameter. Only one team may be associated with the policy.

example.name: Engineering Escalation Policy
example.description: Here is the ep for the engineering team.

incident_number.description: The number of the incident. This is unique across your account.

title.description: A succinct description of the nature, symptoms, cause, or effect of the incident.

created_at.description: The time the incident was first triggered.

updated_at.description: The time the incident was last modified.

status.description: The current status of the incident.

service.description: The service the incident is on. If the `include[]=services` query parameter is provided, the full service definition will be returned.

assignments.description: List of all assignments for this incident. This list will be empty if the `Incident.status` is `resolved`. Returns a user reference for each assignment. Full user definitions will be returned if the `include[]=assignees` query parameter is provided.

assigned_via.description: How the current incident assignments were decided.  Note that `direct_assignment` incidents will not escalate up the attached `escalation_policy`

last_status_change_at.description: The time the status of the incident last changed. If the incident is not currently acknowledged or resolved, this will be the incident's `updated_at`.

resolved_at.description: The time the incident became "resolved" or `null` if the incident is not resolved.

first_trigger_log_entry.description: The first log entry on the incident. The log entry will be of type `TriggerLogEntry` and will represent information about how the incident was triggered. If the `include[]=first_trigger_log_entries` query parameter is provided, the full log entry definition will be returned.

is_mergeable.description: Whether the incident is mergeable. Only incidents that have alerts, or that are manually created can be merged.

incident_type.description: The incident type of the incident.

name.description: The name of the Incident Type.

escalation_policy.description: The escalation policy attached to the service that the incident is on. If the `include[]=escalation_policies` query parameter is provided, the full escalation policy definition will be returned.

teams.description: The teams involved in the incident’s lifecycle. If the `include[]=teams` query parameter is provided, the full team definitions will be returned.

pending_actions.description: The list of pending_actions on the incident. A pending_action object contains a type of action which can be escalate, unacknowledge, resolve or urgency_change. A pending_action object contains at, the time at which the action will take place. An urgency_change pending_action will contain to, the urgency that the incident will change to.

acknowledgements.description: List of all acknowledgements for this incident. This list will be empty if the `Incident.status` is `resolved` or `triggered`. If the `include[]=acknowledgers` query parameter is provided, the full user or service definitions will be returned for each acknowledgement entry.

alert_grouping.description: Describes the alert grouping state of this incident. Will be null if the incident has no alerts.

last_status_change_by.description: The entity that last changed the status of the incident. If the `include[]=agents` query parameter is provided, the full user/service/integration definition will be returned.

conference_bridge.description: The conference bridge information attached to the incident. Only returned if the `include[]=conference_bridge` query parameter is provided.

incidents_responders.description: The responders on the incident. Only returned if the account has access to the [responder requests](https://support.pagerduty.com/docs/add-responders) feature.

responder_requests.description: Previous responder requests made on this incident. Only returned if the account has access to the [responder requests](https://support.pagerduty.com/docs/add-responders) feature.

urgency.description: The current urgency of the incident.

body.description: The additional incident body details. Only returned if the `include[]=body` query parameter is provided.

name.description: The name of the Incident Type.

conference_number.description: The phone number of the conference call for the conference bridge. Phone numbers should be formatted like +1 415-555-1212,,,,1234#, where a comma (,) represents a one-second wait and pound (#) completes access code input.

conference_url.description: An URL for the conference bridge. This could be a link to a web conference or Slack channel.

schedule.description: The schedule from which this on-call originates. May be a legacy schedule reference or a v3 schedule reference.

escalation_level.description: The escalation level for the on-call.

start.description: The start of the on-call. If `null`, the on-call is a permanent user on-call.

end.description: The end of the on-call. If `null`, the user does not go off-call.

type.description: The type of object being created.

time_zone.description: The time zone of the schedule.

escalation_policies.description: An array of all of the escalation policies that uses this schedule.

users.description: An array of all of the users on the schedule.

teams.description: An array of all of the teams on the schedule.

start.description: The start date for the User shift

end.description: The end date for the User shift

type.description: The type of object being created.

description.description: The user-provided description of the service.

auto_resolve_timeout.description: Time in seconds that an incident is automatically resolved if left open for that long. Value is `null` if the feature is disabled. Value must not be negative. Setting this field to `0`, `null` (or unset in POST request) will disable the feature.

acknowledgement_timeout.description: Time in seconds that an incident changes to the Triggered State after being Acknowledged. Value is `null` if the feature is disabled. Value must not be negative. Setting this field to `0`, `null` (or unset in POST request) will disable the feature.

created_at.description: The date/time when this service was created

status.description:
The current state of the Service. Valid statuses are:


- `active`: The service is enabled and has no open incidents. This is the only status a service can be created with.
- `warning`: The service is enabled and has one or more acknowledged incidents.
- `critical`: The service is enabled and has one or more triggered incidents.
- `maintenance`: The service is under maintenance, no new incidents will be triggered during maintenance mode.
- `disabled`: The service is disabled and will not have any new triggered incidents.

last_incident_timestamp.description: The date/time when the most recent incident was created for this service.

response_play.description: Response plays associated with this service.

teams.description: The set of teams associated with this service.

integrations.description: An array containing Integration objects that belong to this service. If `integrations` is passed as an argument, these are full objects - otherwise, these are references.

scheduled_actions.description: An array containing scheduled actions for the service.

addons.description: The array of Add-ons associated with this service.

alert_creation.description:
Whether a service creates only incidents, or both alerts and incidents. A service must create alerts in order to enable incident merging.
* "create_incidents" - The service will create one incident and zero alerts for each incoming event.
* "create_alerts_and_incidents" - The service will create one incident and one associated alert for each incoming event.
This attribute has been deprecated as all services will be migrated to use alerts and incidents. Afterward, the incident only service setting will no longer be available. For details, please refer to the knowledge base: https://support.pagerduty.com/docs/alerts#enable-and-disable-alerts-on-a-service.

oneOf.title: Alert Grouping Settings Reference
oneOf.description: When a service uses alert grouping configuration that is unsupported via the services api, and can only be configured via the [Alert Grouping Settings API](https://developer.pagerduty.com/api-reference/587edbc8ff416-create-an-alert-grouping-setting). The reference object includes the new location details for the service's Alert Grouping Setting. When an `alert_grouping_settings_reference` is included in a create or update request it will be ignored and no changes are applied to the service.

id.description: id of the related alert grouping setting

self.description: link to api endpoint for this setting

html_url.description: link to the ui page to edit the setting

alert_grouping_timeout.description:
The duration in minutes within which to automatically group incoming alerts. This setting applies only when `alert_grouping` is set to `time`. To continue grouping alerts until the Incident is resolved, set this value to `0`.

This attribute has been deprecated and configuration via [Alert Grouping Settings](https://developer.pagerduty.com/api-reference/587edbc8ff416-create-an-alert-grouping-setting) resource is encouraged.

example.name: My Web App
example.description: My cool web application that does things.

type.description: The type of object being created.

time_zone.description: The preferred time zone name. If null, the account's time zone will be used.

role.description: The user role. Account must have the `read_only_users` ability to set a user as a `read_only_user` or a `read_only_limited_user`, and must have advanced permissions abilities to set a user as `observer` or `restricted_access`.

avatar_url.description: The URL of the user's avatar.

invitation_sent.description: If true, the user has an outstanding invitation.

created_via_sso.description: If true, the user was created via Single Sign-On (SSO).

teams.description: The list of teams to which the user belongs. Account must have the `teams` ability to set this.

contact_methods.description: The list of contact methods for the user.

notification_rules.description: The list of notification rules for the user.

http_cal_url.description:
iCal HTTP feed URL for this user's on-call shifts. Only returned on the `GET /users/{id}` detail endpoint: automatically when viewing your own profile with a user-level token, or when an account admin with `can_update_user` passes `include[]=calendar_urls` for another user. Not returned on list endpoints or with account-level read-only keys.

**Security:** this URL is a bearer credential. Anyone with the URL can read the user's on-call calendar without further authentication. Rotate via `POST /users/{id}/regenerate_private_url_key` if it may have been exposed.

web_cal_url.description:
iCal webcal URL for this user's on-call shifts. Only returned on the `GET /users/{id}` detail endpoint: automatically when viewing your own profile with a user-level token, or when an account admin with `can_update_user` passes `include[]=calendar_urls` for another user. Not returned on list endpoints or with account-level read-only keys.

**Security:** this URL is a bearer credential. Anyone with the URL can read the user's on-call calendar without further authentication. Rotate via `POST /users/{id}/regenerate_private_url_key` if it may have been exposed.

summary.description: A short-form, server-generated string that provides succinct, important information about an object suitable for primary labeling of an entity in a client. In many cases, this will be identical to `name`, though it is not intended to be an identifier.

type.description: A string that determines the schema of the object. This must be the standard name for the entity, suffixed by `_reference` if the object is a reference.

self.description: the API show URL at which the object is accessible

html_url.description: a URL at which the entity is uniquely displayed in the Web app

type.description: The type of object being created.

escalation_delay_in_minutes.description: The number of minutes before an unacknowledged incident escalates away from this rule.

targets.description: The targets an incident should be assigned to upon reaching this rule.

escalation_rule_assignment_strategy.description: The strategy used to assign the escalation rule to an incident.

at.description: Time at which the assignment was created.

triggered.description: The count of triggered alerts grouped into this incident

resolved.description: The count of resolved alerts grouped into this incident

all.description: The total count of alerts grouped into this incident

type.description: The type of object being created.

default_role.description: The team is private if the value is "none", or public if it is "manager" (the default permissions for a non-member of the team are either "none", or their base role up until "manager").

IncidentAction.description: An incident action is a pending change to an incident that will automatically happen at some future time.

to.description: The urgency that the incident will change to. This field is only present when the type is `urgency_change`.

at.description: Time at which the acknowledgement was created.

allOf.description: The agent (user, service or integration) that created or modified the Incident Log Entry.

name.description: The user-provided short name of the priority.

description.description: The user-provided description of the priority.

type.description: The reason the incident was resolved. The only reason currently supported is merge.

state.description: The status of the responder being added to the incident

message.description: The message sent with the responder request

escalation_policy_requests.description: Names of escalation policies that this responder was requested through, if applicable

id.description: The ID of the responder request

requested_at.description: The time the request was made

message.description: The message sent with the responder request

responder_request_targets.description: The array of targets the responder request is being sent to

V3ScheduleReference.description:
Lightweight schedule object returned by the list endpoint.
Uses `"type": "schedule_v3_reference"` to distinguish from
legacy schedules (`"type": "schedule_reference"`).

start.description: The start time of this layer.

end.description: The end time of this layer. If `null`, the layer does not end.

users.description: The ordered list of users on this layer. The position of the user on the list determines their order in the layer.

restrictions.description: An array of restrictions for the layer. A restriction is a limit on which period of the day or week the schedule layer can accept assignments. Restrictions respect the `time_zone` parameter of the request.

rotation_virtual_start.description: The effective start time of the layer. This can be before the start time of the schedule.

rotation_turn_length_seconds.description: The duration of each on-call shift in seconds.

name.description: The name of the schedule layer.

rendered_schedule_entries.description: This is a list of entries on the computed layer for the current time range. Since or until must be set in order for this field to be populated.

rendered_coverage_percentage.description: The percentage of the time range covered by this layer. Returns null unless since or until are set.

rendered_schedule_entries.description: This is a list of entries on the computed layer for the current time range. Since or until must be set in order for this field to be populated.

rendered_coverage_percentage.description: The percentage of the time range covered by this layer. Returns null unless since or until are set.

time_zone.description: The time zone for the support hours

items.description: The days of the week (1 through 7, for Monday through Sunday)

start_time.description: The support hours' starting time of day (date portion is ignored)

end_time.description: The support hours' ending time of day (date portion is ignored)

type.description: The type of schedule action. Must be set to urgency_change.

at.description: Represents when scheduled action will occur.

name.description: Designates either the start or the end of support hours.

to_urgency.description: Urgency level. Must be set to high.

src.description: The URL source of the Addon

name.description: The user entered name of the Addon.

AlertGroupingParameters.title: Alert Grouping Parameters
AlertGroupingParameters.description:
Defines how alerts on this service will be automatically grouped into incidents. Note that the alert grouping features are available only on certain plans. To turn grouping off set the type to null.
This attribute has been deprecated and configuration via [Alert Grouping Settings](https://developer.pagerduty.com/api-reference/587edbc8ff416-create-an-alert-grouping-setting) resource is encouraged.

AutoPauseNotificationsParameters.title: AutoPauseNotificationsParameters
AutoPauseNotificationsParameters.description: Defines how alerts on this service are automatically suspended for a period of time before triggering, when identified as likely being transient. Note that automatically pausing notifications is only available on certain plans.

enabled.description: Indicates whether alerts should be automatically suspended when identified as transient

timeout.description: Indicates in seconds how long alerts should be suspended before triggering. To automatically select the recommended timeout for a service, set this value to `0`.

recommended_timeout.description: The recommended timeout setting for this service based on prior alert patterns.

allOf.description: The escalation target is the entity that will be assigned an incident upon escalation.

created_at.description: Time at which the log entry was created.

note.description: Optional field containing a note, if one was included with the log entry.

contexts.description: Contexts to be included with the trigger such as links to graphs or images.

teams.description: Will consist of references unless included

changeset.description: String record of custom field updates

allOf.description: The acknowledger represents the entity that made the acknowledgement for an incident.

type.description: The type of target (either a user or an escalation policy)

id.description: The id of the user or escalation policy

incident_responders.description: An array of responders associated with the specified incident

duration_seconds.description: The duration of the restriction in seconds.

start_time_of_day.description: The start time in HH:mm:ss format.

start_day_of_week.description: Only required for use with a `weekly_restriction` restriction type. The first day of the weekly rotation schedule as [ISO 8601 day](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_week_date) (1 is Monday, etc.)

start.description: The start time of this entry.

end.description: The end time of this entry. If null, the entry does not end.

type.description: The type of incident urgency: whether it's constant, or it's dependent on the support hours.

urgency.description: The incidents' urgency, if type is constant.

FlexibleTimeWindowIntelligentAlertGroupi.title: Intelligent Alert Grouping
FlexibleTimeWindowIntelligentAlertGroupi.description: The configuration for Intelligent Alert Grouping. Note that this configuration is only available for certain plans.

time_window.description: The maximum amount of time allowed between Alerts. Any Alerts arriving greater than `time_window` seconds apart will not be grouped together. This is a rolling time window and is counted from the most recently grouped alert. The window is extended every time a new alert is added to the group, up to 24 hours. To use the "recommended_time_window," set the value to 0, otherwise the value must be between 300 and 3600.

recommended_time_window.description: In order to ensure your Service has the optimal grouping window, we use data science to calculate your Service`s average Alert inter-arrival time. We encourage customer`s to use this value, please set `time_window` to 0 to use the `recommended_time_window`.

TimeBasedAlertGroupingConfiguration.title: Time Grouping
TimeBasedAlertGroupingConfiguration.description: The configuration for Time Based Alert Grouping

timeout.description: The duration in minutes within which to automatically group incoming Alerts. To continue grouping Alerts until the Incident is resolved, set this value to 0.

ContentBasedAlertGroupingConfiguration.title: Content Only Grouping
ContentBasedAlertGroupingConfiguration.description: The configuration for Content Based Alert Grouping

aggregate.description: Whether Alerts should be grouped if `all` or `any` specified fields match. If `all` is selected, an exact match on every specified field name must occur for Alerts to be grouped. If `any` is selected, Alerts will be grouped when there is an exact match on at least one of the specified fields.

fields.description: An array of strings which represent the fields with which to group against. Depending on the aggregate, Alerts will group if some or all the fields match.

time_window.description: The maximum amount of time allowed between Alerts. Any Alerts arriving greater than `time_window` seconds apart will not be grouped together. This is a rolling time window up to 24 hours and is counted from the most recently grouped alert. The window is extended every time a new alert is added to the group, up to 24 hours (24 hours only applies to single-service settings). To use the "recommended_time_window," set the value to 0, otherwise the value must be between 300 <= time_window <= 3600 or 86400(i.e. 24 hours).

recommended_time_window.description: In order to ensure your Service has the optimal grouping window, we use data science to calculate your Service's average Alert inter-arrival time. We encourage customer's to use this value, please set `time_window` to 0 to use the `recommended_time_window`.

Channel.description: Polymorphic object representation of the means by which the action was channeled. Has different formats depending on type, indicated by channel[type]. Will be one of `auto`, `email`, `api`, `nagios`, or `timeout` if `agent[type]` is `service`. Will be one of `email`, `sms`, `website`, `web_trigger`, or `note` if `agent[type]` is `user`.

changeset.description: Changeset present in CustomFieldsValueChange and FieldValueChange log entries.

customer_fields.description: Customer Fields present in CustomFieldsValueChange and FieldValueChange log entries.

application_fields.description: Application Fields present in CustomFieldsValueChange and FieldValueChange log entries.

service.description: Nagios service that created the event, if applicable

body_content_type.description: Content type of the email body. Will be `text/plain` or `text/html`

raw_url.description: URL for raw text of email

html_url.description: URL for html rendered version of the email. Only present if `content_type` is `text/html`

duration.description: For `snooze` log entries, this is the number of seconds that the incident was snoozed for.

type.description: The type of context being attached to the incident.

text.description: The alternate display for an image

started_at.description: The time at which the notification was sent

address.description: The address where the notification was sent. This will be null for notification type `push_notification`.

conferenceAddress.description: The address of the conference bridge

ArgumentError.description: Caller provided invalid arguments. Please review the response for error details. Retrying with the same arguments will *not* work.

Unauthorized.description:
Caller did not supply credentials or did not provide the correct credentials.
If you are using an API key, it may be invalid or your Authorization header may be malformed.

Forbidden.description:
Caller is not authorized to view the requested resource.
While your authentication is valid, the authenticated user or token does not have permission to perform this action.

TooManyRequests.description: Too many requests have been made, the rate limit has been reached.

NotFound.description: The requested resource was not found.

PaymentRequired.description:
Account does not have the abilities to perform the action. Please review the response for the required abilities.
You can also use the [Abilities API](#resource_Abilities) to determine what features are available to your account.

Conflict.description: The request conflicts with the current state of the server.

schema.description: Generic error response from the PagerDuty API
